content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * Integration tests for Account Deploy Flow — account-deploy category.
     3→ *
     4→ * Tests the 5-step mainnet deployment flow:
     5→ *   1. generating-keypair  (Falcon keygen via FalconService)
     6→ *   2. packing             (pack public key to felt252 slots)
     7→ *   3. computing-address   (pure hash + CallData computation)
     8→ *   4. awaiting-funds      (balance check loop via getBalance)
     9→ *   5. deploying           (broadcastAccount + waitForTx)
    10→ *
    11→ * Strategy:
    12→ * - All StarknetService calls use Layer.succeed mocks — no live RPC
    13→ * - Atoms are driven via Registry.make() for isolated state tracking
    14→ * - An orchestrated Effect drives state transitions the same way
    15→ *   AccountDeployFlow component will drive them in production
    16→ * - Every service method has success + failure coverage
    17→ * - Every Schema.TaggedError appears in at least one failure case
    18→ *
    19→ * Coverage requirements:
    20→ * - computeDeployAddress: success, failure
    21→ * - getBalance: success (funded), success (zero = awaiting funds), failure
    22→ * - deployAccount: success, failure (no txHash), failure (with txHash)
    23→ * - waitForTx: success, failure
    24→ * - InsufficientFundsError: constructable, matchable, catchTag
    25→ * - AccountDeployError: with/without txHash in deploy context
    26→ * - Orchestrated flow: full happy-path, error propagation
    27→ */
    28→
    29→import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach } from "bun:test"
    30→import { Effect, Exit, Layer, Option, Cause, ConfigProvider } from "effect"
    31→import { Registry } from "@effect-atom/atom"
    32→
    33→import { StarknetService } from "../../services/StarknetService"
    34→import {
    35→  StarknetRpcError,
    36→  AccountDeployError,
    37→  InsufficientFundsError,
    38→} from "../../services/errors"
    39→import {
    40→  TxHash,
    41→  ContractAddress,
    42→} from "../../services/types"
    43→import type { PackedPublicKey } from "../../services/types"
    44→import type { DeployStep } from "../../atoms/starknet"
    45→import {
    46→  deployStepAtom,
    47→  deployedAddressAtom,
    48→  deployTxHashAtom,
    49→} from "../../atoms/starknet"
    50→
    51→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    52→// Test fixtures
    53→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    54→
    55→const MOCK_ADDRESS = ContractAddress.make("0x049d36570d4e46f48e99674bd3fcc84644ddd6b96f7c741b1562b82f9e004dc7")
    56→const MOCK_TX_HASH = TxHash.make("0x7f3e2a1b9c4d5e6f0a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b")
    57→const MOCK_PACKED_PK: PackedPublicKey = {
    58→  slots: Array.from({ length: 29 }, (_, i) => `0x${i.toString(16).padStart(64, "0")}`),
    59→}
    60→const MOCK_PRIVATE_KEY = "0xdeadbeefcafebabe1234567890abcdef"
    61→const FUNDED_BALANCE = BigInt("1000000000000000000") // 1 STRK
    62→const ZERO_BALANCE = BigInt(0)
    63→
    64→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    65→// Mock Layer builders
    66→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    67→
    68→/**
    69→ * Happy-path StarknetService: all methods succeed.
    70→ */
    71→const makeSuccessLayer = (): Layer.Layer<StarknetService> =>
    72→  Layer.succeed(StarknetService, {
    73→    computeDeployAddress: (_pk: PackedPublicKey) =>
    74→      Effect.succeed(MOCK_ADDRESS),
    75→    getBalance: (_addr: string) =>
    76→      Effect.succeed(FUNDED_BALANCE),
    77→    deployAccount: (_pk: PackedPublicKey, _sk: string) =>
    78→      Effect.succeed({ txHash: MOCK_TX_HASH, address: MOCK_ADDRESS }),
    79→    waitForTx: (_tx: string) =>
    80→      Effect.succeed(undefined as void),
    81→    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    82→    provider: {} as any,
    83→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    84→  } as any)
    85→
    86→/**
    87→ * Zero-balance service: getBalance returns 0 (account not yet funded).
    88→ */
    89→const makeZeroBalanceLayer = (): Layer.Layer<StarknetService> =>
    90→  Layer.succeed(StarknetService, {
    91→    computeDeployAddress: (_pk: PackedPublicKey) =>
    92→      Effect.succeed(MOCK_ADDRESS),
    93→    getBalance: (_addr: string) =>
    94→      Effect.succeed(ZERO_BALANCE),
    95→    deployAccount: (_pk: PackedPublicKey, _sk: string) =>
    96→      Effect.fail(new AccountDeployError({ message: "should not be reached" })),
    97→    waitForTx: (_tx: string) =>
    98→      Effect.succeed(undefined as void),
    99→    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
   100→    provider: {} as any,
   101→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
   102→  } as any)
   103→
   104→/**
   105→ * Deploy failure: deployAccount fails without txHash.
   106→ */
   107→const makeDeployFailureLayer = (): Layer.Layer<StarknetService> =>
   108→  Layer.succeed(StarknetService, {
   109→    computeDeployAddress: (_pk: PackedPublicKey) =>
   110→      Effect.succeed(MOCK_ADDRESS),
   111→    getBalance: (_addr: string) =>
   112→      Effect.succeed(FUNDED_BALANCE),
   113→    deployAccount: (_pk: PackedPublicKey, _sk: string) =>
   114→      Effect.fail(new AccountDeployError({ message: "insufficient fee" })),
   115→    waitForTx: (_tx: string) =>
   116→      Effect.succeed(undefined as void),
   117→    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
   118→    provider: {} as any,
   119→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
   120→  } as any)
   121→
   122→/**
   123→ * Deploy failure: deployAccount fails WITH a txHash (tx was broadcast but reverted).
   124→ */
   125→const makeDeployRevertedLayer = (): Layer.Layer<StarknetService> =>
   126→  Layer.succeed(StarknetService, {
   127→    computeDeployAddress: (_pk: PackedPublicKey) =>
   128→      Effect.succeed(MOCK_ADDRESS),
   129→    getBalance: (_addr: string) =>
   130→      Effect.succeed(FUNDED_BALANCE),
   131→    deployAccount: (_pk: PackedPublicKey, _sk: string) =>
   132→      Effect.fail(
   133→        new AccountDeployError({
   134→          message: "transaction reverted",
   135→          txHash: MOCK_TX_HASH,
   136→        }),
   137→      ),
   138→    waitForTx: (_tx: string) =>
   139→      Effect.succeed(undefined as void),
   140→    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
   141→    provider: {} as any,
   142→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
   143→  } as any)
   144→
   145→/**
   146→ * RPC failure: computeDeployAddress fails (simulates RPC down at address computation).
   147→ */
   148→const makeRpcFailureLayer = (): Layer.Layer<StarknetService> =>
   149→  Layer.succeed(StarknetService, {
   150→    computeDeployAddress: (_pk: PackedPublicKey) =>
   151→      Effect.fail(new StarknetRpcError({ message: "connection refused", code: -1 })),
   152→    getBalance: (_addr: string) =>
   153→      Effect.fail(new StarknetRpcError({ message: "connection refused", code: -1 })),
   154→    deployAccount: (_pk: PackedPublicKey, _sk: string) =>
   155→      Effect.fail(new AccountDeployError({ message: "connection refused" })),
   156→    waitForTx: (_tx: string) =>
   157→      Effect.fail(new StarknetRpcError({ message: "timeout", code: -32003 })),
   158→    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
   159→    provider: {} as any,
   160→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
   161→  } as any)
   162→
   163→/** Run an effect provided with a specific StarknetService mock layer */
   164→async function runWith<A, E>(
   165→  effect: Effect.Effect<A, E, StarknetService>,
   166→  serviceLayer: Layer.Layer<StarknetService>,
   167→): Promise<Exit.Exit<A, E>> {
   168→  return Effect.runPromiseExit(effect.pipe(Effect.provide(serviceLayer)))
   169→}

   171→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   172→// Group 1: DeployStep state machine — atom transitions
   173→// (Complements atoms.test.ts; tests account-deploy-specific scenarios)
   174→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   175→
   176→describe("DeployStep state machine — account-deploy scenarios", () => {
   177→  it("idle → awaiting-funds preserves address in step payload", () => {
   178→    const registry = Registry.make()
   179→    registry.set(deployStepAtom, { step: "awaiting-funds", address: MOCK_ADDRESS })
   180→    const state = registry.get(deployStepAtom)
   181→    expect(state.step).toBe("awaiting-funds")
   182→    expect((state as Extract<DeployStep, { step: "awaiting-funds" }>).address).toBe(MOCK_ADDRESS)
   183→  })
   184→
   185→  it("deploying step preserves address", () => {
   186→    const registry = Registry.make()
   187→    registry.set(deployStepAtom, { step: "deploying", address: MOCK_ADDRESS })
   188→    const state = registry.get(deployStepAtom)
   189→    expect(state.step).toBe("deploying")
   190→    expect((state as Extract<DeployStep, { step: "deploying" }>).address).toBe(MOCK_ADDRESS)
   191→  })
   192→
   193→  it("deployed step carries both address and txHash", () => {
   194→    const registry = Registry.make()
   195→    registry.set(deployStepAtom, {
   196→      step: "deployed",
   197→      address: MOCK_ADDRESS,
   198→      txHash: MOCK_TX_HASH,
   199→    })
   200→    const state = registry.get(deployStepAtom) as Extract<DeployStep, { step: "deployed" }>
   201→    expect(state.step).toBe("deployed")
   202→    expect(state.address).toBe(MOCK_ADDRESS)
   203→    expect(state.txHash).toBe(MOCK_TX_HASH)
   204→  })
   205→
   206→  it("error step carries human-readable message", () => {
   207→    const registry = Registry.make()
   208→    registry.set(deployStepAtom, {
   209→      step: "error",
   210→      message: "Insufficient STRK balance — please fund the account",
   211→    })
   212→    const state = registry.get(deployStepAtom) as Extract<DeployStep, { step: "error" }>
   213→    expect(state.step).toBe("error")
   214→    expect(state.message).toContain("Insufficient")
   215→  })
   216→
   217→  it("subscribe fires on each deploy step transition", () => {
   218→    const registry = Registry.make()
   219→    const steps: string[] = []
   220→    registry.subscribe(deployStepAtom, (v) => steps.push(v.step))
   221→    registry.set(deployStepAtom, { step: "generating-keypair" })
   222→    registry.set(deployStepAtom, { step: "packing" })
   223→    registry.set(deployStepAtom, { step: "computing-address" })
   224→    expect(steps).toEqual(["generating-keypair", "packing", "computing-address"])
   225→  })
   226→
   227→  it("deployedAddressAtom and deployTxHashAtom are set at deployed step", () => {
   228→    const registry = Registry.make()
   229→    // Simulate what AccountDeployFlow component will do on success
   230→    registry.set(deployStepAtom, {
   231→      step: "deployed",
   232→      address: MOCK_ADDRESS,
   233→      txHash: MOCK_TX_HASH,
   234→    })
   235→    registry.set(deployedAddressAtom, Option.some(MOCK_ADDRESS))
   236→    registry.set(deployTxHashAtom, Option.some(MOCK_TX_HASH))
   237→
   238→    expect(Option.isSome(registry.get(deployedAddressAtom))).toBe(true)
   239→    expect(Option.isSome(registry.get(deployTxHashAtom))).toBe(true)
   240→    expect(Option.getOrThrow(registry.get(deployedAddressAtom))).toBe(MOCK_ADDRESS)
   241→    expect(Option.getOrThrow(registry.get(deployTxHashAtom))).toBe(MOCK_TX_HASH)
   242→  })
   243→
   244→  it("error resets deployedAddressAtom and deployTxHashAtom to none", () => {
   245→    const registry = Registry.make()
   246→    // Simulate a re-run after error: clear previous deployment state
   247→    registry.set(deployedAddressAtom, Option.none())
   248→    registry.set(deployTxHashAtom, Option.none())
   249→    registry.set(deployStepAtom, { step: "error", message: "retry" })
   250→
   251→    expect(Option.isNone(registry.get(deployedAddressAtom))).toBe(true)
   252→    expect(Option.isNone(registry.get(deployTxHashAtom))).toBe(true)
   253→  })
   254→})

   259→
   260→describe("StarknetService.computeDeployAddress — account-deploy step 3", () => {
   261→  it("success: returns a 0x-prefixed hex string (ContractAddress)", async () => {
   262→    const exit = await runWith(
   263→      StarknetService.computeDeployAddress(MOCK_PACKED_PK),
   264→      makeSuccessLayer(),
   265→    )
   266→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
   267→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
   268→      expect(typeof exit.value).toBe("string")
   269→      expect((exit.value as string).startsWith("0x")).toBe(true)
   270→    }
   271→  })
   272→
   273→  it("failure: StarknetRpcError carries code and message", async () => {
   274→    const exit = await runWith(
   275→      StarknetService.computeDeployAddress(MOCK_PACKED_PK),
   276→      makeRpcFailureLayer(),
   277→    )
   278→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   279→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
   280→      const failure = Cause.failureOption(exit.cause)
   281→      expect(failure._tag).toBe("Some")
   282→      // @ts-expect-error Option.Some
   283→      const err = failure.value
   284→      expect(err._tag).toBe("StarknetRpcError")
   285→      expect(err.code).toBe(-1)
   286→    }
   287→  })
   288→
   289→  it("real implementation (pure computation — no network): returns deterministic hex address", async () => {
   290→    const withTestConfig = ConfigProvider.fromMap(
   291→      new Map([["NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL", "http://localhost:9999"]]),
   292→    )
   293→    const exit = await Effect.runPromiseExit(
   294→      StarknetService.computeDeployAddress(MOCK_PACKED_PK).pipe(
   295→        Effect.provide(StarknetService.Default),
   296→        Effect.provide(Layer.setConfigProvider(withTestConfig)),
   297→      ),
   298→    )
   299→    // computeDeployAddress is pure (no RPC call), so must succeed
   300→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
   301→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
   302→      expect(typeof exit.value.address).toBe("string")
   303→      expect(exit.value.address.startsWith("0x")).toBe(true)
   304→      expect(typeof exit.value.salt).toBe("string")
   305→      expect(exit.value.salt.startsWith("0x")).toBe(true)
   306→    }
   307→  })
   308→})

   366→describe("StarknetService.deployAccount — step 5", () => {
   367→  it("success: returns { txHash, address } with correct shapes", async () => {
   368→    const exit = await runWith(
   369→      StarknetService.deployAccount(MOCK_PACKED_PK, MOCK_PRIVATE_KEY),

   377→      expect((exit.value.address as string).startsWith("0x")).toBe(true)

   380→
   381→  it("failure: AccountDeployError without txHash (pre-broadcast rejection)", async () => {

   397→  it("failure: AccountDeployError WITH txHash (broadcast succeeded but tx reverted)", async () => {

   405→      // @ts-expect-error Option.Some

   408→      expect(err.txHash).toBe(MOCK_TX_HASH)
   409→      expect(err.message).toBe("transaction reverted")
   410→    }
   411→  })
   412→
   413→  it("AccountDeployError with txHash can be caught and txHash extracted", async () => {
   414→    let caughtTxHash: string | undefined = undefined
   415→    await Effect.runPromise(
   416→      Effect.fail(
   417→        new AccountDeployError({ message: "reverted", txHash: MOCK_TX_HASH }),
   418→      ).pipe(
   419→        Effect.catchTag("AccountDeployError", (e) => {
   420→          caughtTxHash = e.txHash
   421→          return Effect.succeed(null)
   422→        }),
   423→      ),
   424→    )
   425→    // Use non-null assertion: we know caughtTxHash was set by catchTag
   426→    // bun:test toBe() has strict overloads that require matching types
   427→    expect(caughtTxHash!).toBe(MOCK_TX_HASH as string)
   428→  })
   429→})

   466→
   467→describe("InsufficientFundsError — balance gate error type", () => {
   468→  it("is constructable with all required fields", () => {
   469→    const err = new InsufficientFundsError({
   470→      message: "Need STRK to deploy",
   471→      address: MOCK_ADDRESS,
   472→      required: "1000000000000000000",
   473→    })
   474→    expect(err._tag).toBe("InsufficientFundsError")
   475→    expect(err.address).toBe(MOCK_ADDRESS)
   476→    expect(err.required).toBe("1000000000000000000")
   477→    expect(err.message).toBe("Need STRK to deploy")
   478→  })
   479→
   480→  it("roundtrips through Effect.runPromiseExit with correct Exit.isFailure", async () => {
   481→    const exit = await Effect.runPromiseExit(
   482→      Effect.fail(
   483→        new InsufficientFundsError({
   484→          message: "balance too low",
   485→          address: MOCK_ADDRESS,
   486→          required: "500",
   487→        }),
   488→      ),
   489→    )
   490→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   491→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(false)
   492→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
   493→      const failure = Cause.failureOption(exit.cause)
   494→      // @ts-expect-error Option.Some
   495→      expect(failure.value._tag).toBe("InsufficientFundsError")
   496→    }
   497→  })
   498→
   499→  it("can be caught with catchTag and address extracted", async () => {
   500→    let caughtAddress = ""
   501→    let caughtRequired = ""
   502→    await Effect.runPromise(
   503→      Effect.fail(
   504→        new InsufficientFundsError({
   505→          message: "not enough",
   506→          address: MOCK_ADDRESS,
   507→          required: "1000",
   508→        }),
   509→      ).pipe(
   510→        Effect.catchTag("InsufficientFundsError", (e) => {
   511→          caughtAddress = e.address
   512→          caughtRequired = e.required
   513→          return Effect.succeed(null)
   514→        }),
   515→      ),
   516→    )
   517→    expect(caughtAddress).toBe(MOCK_ADDRESS)
   518→    expect(caughtRequired).toBe("1000")
   519→  })
   520→
   521→  it("_tag is 'InsufficientFundsError' (not AccountDeployError)", () => {
   522→    const err = new InsufficientFundsError({
   523→      message: "x",
   524→      address: "0x0",
   525→      required: "0",
   526→    })
   527→    expect(err._tag).toBe("InsufficientFundsError")
   528→    expect(err._tag).not.toBe("AccountDeployError")
   529→    expect(err._tag).not.toBe("StarknetRpcError")
   530→  })
   531→
   532→  it("simulate balance gate: zero balance → InsufficientFundsError", async () => {
   533→    // Simulate what AccountDeployFlow would do:
   534→    // if getBalance returns 0n and user hasn't funded, produce InsufficientFundsError
   535→    const balanceGateEffect = Effect.gen(function* () {
   536→      const balance = yield* StarknetService.getBalance(MOCK_ADDRESS)
   537→      if (balance === ZERO_BALANCE) {
   538→        yield* Effect.fail(
   539→          new InsufficientFundsError({
   540→            message: "Account not funded — send STRK to deploy",
   541→            address: MOCK_ADDRESS,
   542→            required: "1000000000000000000",
   543→          }),
   544→        )
   545→      }
   546→      return balance
   547→    })
   548→
   549→    const exit = await runWith(balanceGateEffect, makeZeroBalanceLayer())
   550→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   551→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
   552→      const failure = Cause.failureOption(exit.cause)
   553→      // @ts-expect-error Option.Some
   554→      expect(failure.value._tag).toBe("InsufficientFundsError")
   555→    }
   556→  })
   557→
   558→  it("simulate balance gate: funded balance → passes through (no error)", async () => {
   559→    const balanceGateEffect = Effect.gen(function* () {
   560→      const balance = yield* StarknetService.getBalance(MOCK_ADDRESS)
   561→      if (balance === ZERO_BALANCE) {
   562→        yield* Effect.fail(
   563→          new InsufficientFundsError({
   564→            message: "not funded",
   565→            address: MOCK_ADDRESS,
   566→            required: "1",
   567→          }),
   568→        )
   569→      }
   570→      return balance
   571→    })
   572→
   573→    const exit = await runWith(balanceGateEffect, makeSuccessLayer())
   574→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
   575→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
   576→      expect(exit.value).toBe(FUNDED_BALANCE)
   577→    }
   578→  })
   579→})
   580→
   581→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   582→// Group 7: Orchestrated 5-step deploy flow Effect
   583→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   584→
   585→/**
   586→ * Orchestrates the full 5-step AccountDeployFlow using the StarknetService.
   587→ * This is how AccountDeployFlow.tsx will drive the deploy — we test the
   588→ * Effect pipeline here without a React component.
   589→ *
   590→ * Steps:
   591→ * 1. generating-keypair  (not in StarknetService — handled by FalconService; mocked here)
   592→ * 2. packing             (not in StarknetService — handled by FalconService; mocked here)
   593→ * 3. computing-address   (StarknetService.computeDeployAddress)
   594→ * 4. awaiting-funds      (StarknetService.getBalance — stays in step until > 0)
   595→ * 5. deploying           (StarknetService.deployAccount)
   596→ */
   597→/**
   598→ * Returns the named step sequence the flow visited (via subscribe callback array).
   599→ * We only subscribe to deployStepAtom; the Effect.gen drives state via registry.set
   600→ * for that atom only. Option-typed atoms (deployedAddressAtom, deployTxHashAtom) are
   601→ * set OUTSIDE the Effect by the component after the effect returns — we test that
   602→ * separately in Groups 1 and 8.
   603→ */
   604→const makeDeployOrchestrationEffect = (registry: ReturnType<typeof Registry.make>) =>
   605→  Effect.gen(function* () {
   606→    // Step 1: generating-keypair (simulated — FalconService handled separately)
   607→    registry.set(deployStepAtom, { step: "generating-keypair" })
   608→
   609→    // Step 2: packing
   610→    registry.set(deployStepAtom, { step: "packing" })
   611→    const packedPk = MOCK_PACKED_PK
   612→
   613→    // Step 3: computing-address
   614→    registry.set(deployStepAtom, { step: "computing-address" })
   615→    const address = yield* StarknetService.computeDeployAddress(packedPk)
   616→
   617→    // Step 4: awaiting-funds
   618→    registry.set(deployStepAtom, { step: "awaiting-funds", address })
   619→    const balance = yield* StarknetService.getBalance(address)
   620→
   621→    // Step 5: deploying (only if funded)
   622→    registry.set(deployStepAtom, { step: "deploying", address })
   623→    const result = yield* StarknetService.deployAccount(packedPk, MOCK_PRIVATE_KEY)
   624→
   625→    // Done — component sets Option atoms after effect resolves (see Group 1 & 8 tests)
   626→    registry.set(deployStepAtom, {
   627→      step: "deployed",
   628→      address: result.address,
   629→      txHash: result.txHash,
   630→    })
   631→
   632→    return { address: result.address, txHash: result.txHash, balance }
   633→  })
   634→
   635→describe("Orchestrated 5-step deploy flow", () => {
   636→  it("happy path: effect returns address+txHash and subscribe shows full step sequence", async () => {
   637→    const registry = Registry.make()
   638→    const steps: string[] = []
   639→    registry.subscribe(deployStepAtom, (v) => steps.push(v.step))
   640→
   641→    const exit = await Effect.runPromiseExit(
   642→      makeDeployOrchestrationEffect(registry).pipe(
   643→        Effect.provide(makeSuccessLayer()),
   644→      ),
   645→    )
   646→
   647→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
   648→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
   649→      expect(exit.value.address).toBe(MOCK_ADDRESS)
   650→      expect(exit.value.txHash).toBe(MOCK_TX_HASH)
   651→      expect(exit.value.balance).toBe(FUNDED_BALANCE)
   652→    }
   653→
   654→    // Verify step sequence was followed (subscribe captures all transitions)
   655→    expect(steps).toContain("generating-keypair")
   656→    expect(steps).toContain("packing")
   657→    expect(steps).toContain("computing-address")
   658→    expect(steps).toContain("awaiting-funds")
   659→    expect(steps).toContain("deploying")
   660→    expect(steps).toContain("deployed")
   661→
   662→    // Component sets Option atoms after effect resolves (simulated here)
   663→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
   664→      registry.set(deployedAddressAtom, Option.some(exit.value.address))
   665→      registry.set(deployTxHashAtom, Option.some(exit.value.txHash))
   666→      expect(Option.isSome(registry.get(deployedAddressAtom))).toBe(true)
   667→      expect(Option.isSome(registry.get(deployTxHashAtom))).toBe(true)
   668→    }
   669→  })
   670→
   671→  it("RPC failure at computeDeployAddress: exit is Failure with StarknetRpcError", async () => {
   672→    const registry = Registry.make()
   673→    const steps: string[] = []
   674→    registry.subscribe(deployStepAtom, (v) => steps.push(v.step))
   675→
   676→    const exit = await Effect.runPromiseExit(
   677→      makeDeployOrchestrationEffect(registry).pipe(
   678→        Effect.provide(makeRpcFailureLayer()),
   679→      ),
   680→    )
   681→
   682→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   683→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
   684→      // Effect fails because computeDeployAddress returned StarknetRpcError
   685→      const cause = JSON.stringify(exit.cause)
   686→      expect(cause).toContain("StarknetRpcError")
   687→    }
   688→
   689→    // Deploy flow reached computing-address step before failing
   690→    // (subscribe captures all steps set before the failing yield*)
   691→    expect(steps).toContain("computing-address")
   692→    expect(steps).not.toContain("awaiting-funds")
   693→    expect(steps).not.toContain("deploying")
   694→    expect(steps).not.toContain("deployed")
   695→  })
   696→
   697→  it("deploy rejection: AccountDeployError at step 5, subscribe shows deploying step reached", async () => {
   698→    const registry = Registry.make()
   699→    const steps: string[] = []
   700→    registry.subscribe(deployStepAtom, (v) => steps.push(v.step))
   701→
   702→    const exit = await Effect.runPromiseExit(
   703→      makeDeployOrchestrationEffect(registry).pipe(
   704→        Effect.provide(makeDeployFailureLayer()),
   705→      ),
   706→    )
   707→
   708→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   709→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
   710→      const failure = Cause.failureOption(exit.cause)
   711→      // @ts-expect-error Option.Some — access value without narrowing for brevity
   712→      expect(failure.value._tag).toBe("AccountDeployError")
   713→      // Use toBe(undefined) instead of toBeUndefined() — bun:test's toBeUndefined()
   714→      // overloads are strict and require the type to be `undefined`, not `string | undefined`
   715→      // @ts-expect-error Option.Some
   716→      expect(failure.value.txHash === undefined).toBe(true)
   717→    }
   718→
   719→    // Steps up to deploying were reached (address computation succeeded)
   720→    expect(steps).toContain("computing-address")
   721→    expect(steps).toContain("awaiting-funds")
   722→    expect(steps).toContain("deploying")
   723→    // But deployed was never reached (deployAccount failed)
   724→    expect(steps).not.toContain("deployed")
   725→  })
   726→
   727→  it("registry isolation: two concurrent deploy flows don't share state", async () => {
   728→    const registry1 = Registry.make()
   729→    const registry2 = Registry.make()
   730→
   731→    registry1.set(deployStepAtom, { step: "deploying", address: ContractAddress.make("0x1111") })
   732→    registry2.set(deployStepAtom, { step: "computing-address" })
   733→
   734→    expect(registry1.get(deployStepAtom).step).toBe("deploying")
   735→    expect(registry2.get(deployStepAtom).step).toBe("computing-address")
   736→  })
   737→})
   738→
   739→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   740→// Group 8: Branded types (TxHash, ContractAddress) in deploy context
   741→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   742→
   743→describe("Branded types in account-deploy flow", () => {
   744→  it("MOCK_ADDRESS and MOCK_TX_HASH are assignable to ContractAddress/TxHash", () => {
   745→    // These are the types that flow through the deploy pipeline
   746→    const addr: ContractAddress = MOCK_ADDRESS
   747→    const tx: TxHash = MOCK_TX_HASH
   748→    expect(typeof addr).toBe("string")
   749→    expect(typeof tx).toBe("string")
   750→    expect(addr.startsWith("0x")).toBe(true)
   751→    expect(tx.startsWith("0x")).toBe(true)
   752→  })
   753→
   754→  it("deployed step shape has address:ContractAddress and txHash:TxHash", () => {
   755→    const registry = Registry.make()
   756→    registry.set(deployStepAtom, {
   757→      step: "deployed",
   758→      address: MOCK_ADDRESS,
   759→      txHash: MOCK_TX_HASH,
   760→    })
   761→    const state = registry.get(deployStepAtom) as Extract<DeployStep, { step: "deployed" }>
   762→    // Both are string-based branded types — no cast needed after DeployStep uses brands
   763→    const address: ContractAddress = state.address
   764→    const txHash: TxHash = state.txHash
   765→    expect(address).toBe(MOCK_ADDRESS)
   766→    expect(txHash).toBe(MOCK_TX_HASH)
   767→  })
   768→
   769→  it("deployedAddressAtom holds Option<ContractAddress>", () => {
   770→    const registry = Registry.make()
   771→    registry.set(deployedAddressAtom, Option.some(MOCK_ADDRESS))
   772→    const val = registry.get(deployedAddressAtom)
   773→    expect(Option.isSome(val)).toBe(true)
   774→    const addr: ContractAddress = Option.getOrThrow(val)
   775→    expect(addr).toBe(MOCK_ADDRESS)
   776→  })
   777→
   778→  it("deployTxHashAtom holds Option<TxHash>", () => {
   779→    const registry = Registry.make()
   780→    registry.set(deployTxHashAtom, Option.some(MOCK_TX_HASH))
   781→    const val = registry.get(deployTxHashAtom)
   782→    expect(Option.isSome(val)).toBe(true)
   783→    const txHash: TxHash = Option.getOrThrow(val)
   784→    expect(txHash).toBe(MOCK_TX_HASH)
   785→  })
   786→})
   787→

Current git branch: frontend
Tech stack: Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript, Effect-TS, Bun test runner

## Task: Run Full Test Suite and Fix All Failures

### Known pre-existing issues to fix

2. **Type errors in test files**: There are type errors in `account-deploy-flow.test.ts` and `starknet-service.test.ts` related to the salt refactor (Task 4). These need to be fixed.

### Step 1: Run tests and capture output


### Step 2: Run typecheck and capture output

Read the failing tests and type errors. Fix them:

For type errors in test files:

- The `computeDeployAddress` mock should return `{ address: MOCK_ADDRESS, salt: "0xdeadbeef" }` instead of just `MOCK_ADDRESS`
- Any `deployAccountEffect` or `deployAccount` calls in tests need to include the `salt` parameter
- Fix each error carefully


### Step 6: Run production build

Expected: Build succeeds.

### Step 7: Commit any fixes

If you changed test files:
```bash

git add apps/demo/src/__tests__/
git commit -m "test(demo): fix tests for Sepolia network switch and salt-reuse refactor"
```

If you changed other files too (e.g., type files), add them separately.

- [ ] No typecheck errors

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * Tests for StarknetService — effect-services category.
     3→ *
     4→ * Strategy: StarknetService is tested via Layer.succeed mocks so no live
     5→ * RPC connection is needed. This covers the contract (method signatures,
     6→ * error types, Exit values) without hitting mainnet.
     7→ *
     8→ * Additionally, the real StarknetService is tested for computeDeployAddress
     9→ * (pure computation — no network call) using ConfigProvider to inject the
    10→ * RPC URL.
    11→ *
    12→ * Coverage requirements:
    13→ * - Every service method must have at least one success and one failure test
    14→ * - StarknetRpcError, AccountDeployError must be producible by the service
    15→ */
    16→
    17→import { describe, it, expect } from "bun:test"
    18→import { Effect, Exit, Layer, ConfigProvider } from "effect"
    19→import { StarknetService } from "../../services/StarknetService"
    20→import { StarknetRpcError, AccountDeployError } from "../../services/errors"
    21→import type { PackedPublicKey, TxHash, ContractAddress } from "../../services/types"
    22→
    23→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24→// Mock StarknetService helpers
    25→// Use `as any` for mock service objects because test mocks intentionally
    26→// include error-producing methods that differ from the inferred success-only types.
    27→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    28→
    29→const mockAddress = "0x049d36570d4e46f48e99674bd3fcc84644ddd6b96f7c741b1562b82f9e004dc7" as ContractAddress
    30→const mockTxHash = "0x7f3e2a1b9c4d5e6f" as TxHash
    31→
    32→// Success mock: all methods return successful Effects
    33→// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    34→const makeSuccessService = (): Layer.Layer<StarknetService> =>
    35→  Layer.succeed(StarknetService, {
    36→    computeDeployAddress: (_pk: PackedPublicKey) =>
    37→      Effect.succeed(mockAddress),
    38→    getBalance: (_addr: string) => Effect.succeed(BigInt("1000000000000000000")),
    39→    deployAccount: (_pk: PackedPublicKey, _sk: string) =>
    40→      Effect.succeed({ txHash: mockTxHash, address: mockAddress }),
    41→    waitForTx: (_tx: string) => Effect.succeed(undefined as void),
    42→    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    43→    provider: {} as any,
    44→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    45→  } as any)
    46→
    47→// Failing mock: all methods return failing Effects with typed errors
    48→// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    49→const makeFailingService = (): Layer.Layer<StarknetService> =>
    50→  Layer.succeed(StarknetService, {
    51→    computeDeployAddress: (_pk: PackedPublicKey) =>
    52→      Effect.fail(new StarknetRpcError({ message: "mock rpc error", code: -1 })),
    53→    getBalance: (_addr: string) =>
    54→      Effect.fail(new StarknetRpcError({ message: "balance fetch failed", code: -32000 })),
    55→    deployAccount: (_pk: PackedPublicKey, _sk: string) =>
    56→      Effect.fail(new AccountDeployError({ message: "deploy rejected" })),
    57→    waitForTx: (_tx: string) =>
    58→      Effect.fail(new StarknetRpcError({ message: "tx timeout", code: -1 })),
    59→    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    60→    provider: {} as any,
    61→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    62→  } as any)
    63→
    64→/** Run an effect provided with a specific StarknetService mock layer */
    65→async function runWith<A, E>(
    66→  effect: Effect.Effect<A, E, StarknetService>,
    67→  serviceLayer: Layer.Layer<StarknetService>,
    68→): Promise<Exit.Exit<A, E>> {
    69→  return Effect.runPromiseExit(
    70→    effect.pipe(Effect.provide(serviceLayer)),
    71→  )
    72→}
    73→
    74→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75→// computeDeployAddress
    76→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    77→
    78→describe("StarknetService.computeDeployAddress (mocked)", () => {
    79→  const mockPk: PackedPublicKey = {
    80→    slots: Array.from({ length: 29 }, (_, i) => `0x${i.toString(16).padStart(64, "0")}`),
    81→  }
    82→
    83→  it("succeeds and returns a ContractAddress string", async () => {
    84→    const exit = await runWith(
    85→      StarknetService.computeDeployAddress(mockPk),
    86→      makeSuccessService(),
    87→    )
    88→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
    89→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
    90→      expect(typeof exit.value).toBe("string")
    91→      expect(exit.value.startsWith("0x")).toBe(true)
    92→    }
    93→  })
    94→
    95→  it("fails with StarknetRpcError when service fails", async () => {
    96→    const exit = await runWith(
    97→      StarknetService.computeDeployAddress(mockPk),
    98→      makeFailingService(),
    99→    )
   100→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   101→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
   102→      expect(JSON.stringify(exit.cause)).toContain("StarknetRpcError")
   103→    }
   104→  })
   105→})
   106→
   107→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   108→// getBalance
   109→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   110→
   111→describe("StarknetService.getBalance (mocked)", () => {
   112→  it("succeeds and returns a bigint balance", async () => {
   113→    const exit = await runWith(
   114→      StarknetService.getBalance("0x1234"),
   115→      makeSuccessService(),
   116→    )
   117→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
   118→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
   119→      expect(typeof exit.value).toBe("bigint")
   120→      expect(exit.value).toBe(BigInt("1000000000000000000"))
   121→    }
   122→  })
   123→
   124→  it("fails with StarknetRpcError when RPC call fails", async () => {
   125→    const exit = await runWith(
   126→      StarknetService.getBalance("0xdeadbeef"),
   127→      makeFailingService(),
   128→    )
   129→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   130→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
   131→      expect(JSON.stringify(exit.cause)).toContain("StarknetRpcError")
   132→    }
   133→  })
   134→})
   135→
   136→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   137→// deployAccount
   138→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   139→
   140→describe("StarknetService.deployAccount (mocked)", () => {
   141→  const mockPk: PackedPublicKey = {
   142→    slots: Array.from({ length: 29 }, (_, i) => `0x${i.toString(16).padStart(64, "0")}`),
   143→  }
   144→
   145→  it("succeeds and returns txHash and address", async () => {
   146→    const exit = await runWith(
   147→      StarknetService.deployAccount(mockPk, "0xprivkey"),
   148→      makeSuccessService(),
   149→    )
   150→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
   151→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
   152→      expect(exit.value.txHash).toBe(mockTxHash)
   153→      expect(exit.value.address).toBe(mockAddress)
   154→    }
   155→  })
   156→
   157→  it("fails with AccountDeployError when deployment is rejected", async () => {
   158→    const exit = await runWith(
   159→      StarknetService.deployAccount(mockPk, "0xprivkey"),
   160→      makeFailingService(),
   161→    )
   162→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   163→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
   164→      expect(JSON.stringify(exit.cause)).toContain("AccountDeployError")
   165→    }
   166→  })
   167→})
   168→
   169→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   170→// waitForTx
   171→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   172→
   173→describe("StarknetService.waitForTx (mocked)", () => {
   174→  it("succeeds (returns void) when transaction confirms", async () => {
   175→    const exit = await runWith(
   176→      StarknetService.waitForTx("0xabc123"),
   177→      makeSuccessService(),
   178→    )
   179→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
   180→  })
   181→
   182→  it("fails with StarknetRpcError when tx times out", async () => {
   183→    const exit = await runWith(
   184→      StarknetService.waitForTx("0xdeadbeef"),
   185→      makeFailingService(),
   186→    )
   187→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   188→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
   189→      expect(JSON.stringify(exit.cause)).toContain("StarknetRpcError")
   190→    }
   191→  })
   192→})
   193→
   194→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   195→// Real StarknetService: computeDeployAddress (pure computation, no network)
   196→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   197→
   198→describe("StarknetService.computeDeployAddress (real implementation)", () => {
   199→  const mockPk: PackedPublicKey = {
   200→    slots: Array.from({ length: 29 }, (_, i) => `0x${i.toString(16).padStart(64, "0")}`),
   201→  }
   202→
   203→  const withTestConfig = ConfigProvider.fromMap(
   204→    new Map([["NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL", "http://localhost:9999"]]),
   205→  )
   206→
   207→  it("returns a hex string address (pure computation, no RPC call)", async () => {
   208→    const exit = await Effect.runPromiseExit(
   209→      StarknetService.computeDeployAddress(mockPk).pipe(
   210→        Effect.provide(StarknetService.Default),
   211→        Effect.provide(Layer.setConfigProvider(withTestConfig)),
   212→      ),
   213→    )
   214→    // computeDeployAddress is pure (hash + CallData only), should succeed
   215→    // even with a fake RPC URL because no network call is made
   216→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
   217→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
   218→      expect(typeof exit.value.address).toBe("string")
   219→      expect(typeof exit.value.salt).toBe("string")
   220→    }
   221→  })
   222→})
   223→
   224→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   225→// StarknetService: fails when Config is missing
   226→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   227→
   228→describe("StarknetService: initialization failure", () => {
   229→  it("StarknetService.Default fails gracefully when Config is missing", async () => {
   230→    const exit = await Effect.runPromiseExit(
   231→      StarknetService.computeDeployAddress({
   232→        slots: Array.from({ length: 29 }, () => "0x0"),
   233→      }).pipe(
   234→        Effect.provide(StarknetService.Default),
   235→        // Provide an empty ConfigProvider to prevent bun from loading .env.local —
   236→        // this simulates the "no config available" scenario the test describes
   237→        Effect.provide(Layer.setConfigProvider(ConfigProvider.fromMap(new Map()))),
   238→      ),
   239→    )
   240→    // Should fail because NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL is not in the empty config
   241→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   242→  })
   243→})
   244→

content.content:
1→import { Config, Effect } from "effect"
     2→import { RpcProvider, Account, hash, CallData, stark } from "starknet"
     3→import {
     4→  StarknetRpcError,
     5→  AccountDeployError,
     6→} from "./errors"
     7→import { TxHash, ContractAddress } from "./types"
     8→import type { PackedPublicKey } from "./types"
     9→
    10→// FalconAccount class hash — declared on Starknet Sepolia
    11→// TODO: replace with actual class hash after running: ./bin/declare-sepolia.sh --account sepolia-deployer
    12→const FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH = "0x0"
    13→
    14→/** STRK token contract address (same on mainnet and Sepolia) */
    15→export const STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS =
    16→  "0x04718f5a0fc34cc1af16a1cdee98ffb20c31f5cd61d6ab07201858f4287c938d"
    17→
    18→export class StarknetService extends Effect.Service<StarknetService>()(
    19→  "StarknetService",
    20→  {
    21→    accessors: true,
    22→    effect: Effect.gen(function* () {
    23→      const rpcUrl = yield* Config.string("NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL")
    24→      const provider = new RpcProvider({ nodeUrl: rpcUrl })
    25→
    26→      const computeDeployAddress = Effect.fn("Starknet.computeDeployAddress")(
    27→        function* (packedPk: PackedPublicKey) {
    28→          const constructorCalldata = CallData.compile({
    29→            pk_packed: packedPk.slots,
    30→          })
    31→          const salt = stark.randomAddress()
    32→          const address = hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(
    33→            salt,
    34→            FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH,
    35→            constructorCalldata,
    36→            0,
    37→          )
    38→          return {
    39→            address: ContractAddress.make(address),
    40→            salt,
    41→          }
    42→        },
    43→      )
    44→
    45→      const getBalance = Effect.fn("Starknet.getBalance")(
    46→        function* (address: string) {
    47→          return yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    48→            try: async () => {
    49→              const result = await provider.callContract({
    50→                contractAddress: STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS,
    51→                entrypoint: "balanceOf",
    52→                calldata: [address],
    53→              })
    54→              return BigInt(result[0])
    55→            },
    56→            catch: (error) =>
    57→              new StarknetRpcError({ message: String(error), code: -1 }),
    58→          })
    59→        },
    60→      )
    61→
    62→      const deployAccount = Effect.fn("Starknet.deployAccount")(
    63→        function* (
    64→          packedPk: PackedPublicKey,
    65→          privateKey: string,
    66→          salt: string,
    67→        ) {
    68→          const constructorCalldata = CallData.compile({
    69→            pk_packed: packedPk.slots,
    70→          })
    71→          const address = hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(
    72→            salt,
    73→            FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH,
    74→            constructorCalldata,
    75→            0,
    76→          )
    77→
    78→          // starknet v9 Account constructor takes a single AccountOptions object
    79→          const account = new Account({ provider, address, signer: privateKey })
    80→
    81→          const result = yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    82→            try: () =>
    83→              account.deployAccount({
    84→                classHash: FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH,
    85→                constructorCalldata,
    86→                addressSalt: salt,
    87→                contractAddress: address,
    88→              }),
    89→            catch: (error) =>
    90→              new AccountDeployError({ message: String(error) }),
    91→          })
    92→
    93→          yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    94→            try: () => provider.waitForTransaction(result.transaction_hash),
    95→            catch: (error) =>
    96→              new AccountDeployError({
    97→                message: `Tx failed: ${error}`,
    98→                txHash: result.transaction_hash,
    99→              }),
   100→          })
   101→
   102→          return {
   103→            txHash: TxHash.make(result.transaction_hash),
   104→            address: ContractAddress.make(result.contract_address),
   105→          }
   106→        },
   107→      )
   108→
   109→      const waitForTx = Effect.fn("Starknet.waitForTx")(
   110→        function* (txHash: string) {
   111→          yield* Effect.tryPromise({
   112→            try: () => provider.waitForTransaction(txHash),
   113→            catch: (error) =>
   114→              new StarknetRpcError({ message: String(error), code: -1 }),
   115→          })
   116→        },
   117→      )
   118→
   119→      return {
   120→        computeDeployAddress,
   121→        getBalance,
   122→        deployAccount,
   123→        waitForTx,
   124→        provider,
   125→      }
   126→    }),
   127→  },
   128→) {}
   129→

input.old_string:
/**
 * Happy-path StarknetService: all methods succeed.
 */
const makeSuccessLayer = (): Layer.Layer<StarknetService> =>
  Layer.succeed(StarknetService, {
    computeDeployAddress: (_pk: PackedPublicKey) =>
      Effect.succeed(MOCK_ADDRESS),
    getBalance: (_addr: string) =>
      Effect.succeed(FUNDED_BALANCE),
    deployAccount: (_pk: PackedPublicKey, _sk: string) =>
      Effect.succeed({ txHash: MOCK_TX_HASH, address: MOCK_ADDRESS }),
    waitForTx: (_tx: string) =>
      Effect.succeed(undefined as void),
    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    provider: {} as any,
  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
  } as any)
input.new_string:
/**
 * Happy-path StarknetService: all methods succeed.
 */
const makeSuccessLayer = (): Layer.Layer<StarknetService> =>
  Layer.succeed(StarknetService, {
    computeDeployAddress: (_pk: PackedPublicKey) =>
      Effect.succeed({ address: MOCK_ADDRESS, salt: "0xdeadbeef" }),
    getBalance: (_addr: string) =>
      Effect.succeed(FUNDED_BALANCE),
    deployAccount: (_pk: PackedPublicKey, _sk: string, _salt: string) =>

/**
 * RPC failure: computeDeployAddress fails (simulates RPC down at address computation).
 */
const makeRpcFailureLayer = (): Layer.Layer<StarknetService> =>
  Layer.succeed(StarknetService, {
    computeDeployAddress: (_pk: PackedPublicKey) =>
      Effect.fail(new StarknetRpcError({ message: "connection refused", code: -1 })),
    getBalance: (_addr: string) =>
      Effect.fail(new StarknetRpcError({ message: "connection refused", code: -1 })),
    deployAccount: (_pk: PackedPublicKey, _sk: string) =>
      Effect.fail(new AccountDeployError({ message: "connection refused" })),
    waitForTx: (_tx: string) =>
      Effect.fail(new StarknetRpcError({ message: "timeout", code: -32003 })),
    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    provider: {} as any,
  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
  } as any)

  Layer.succeed(StarknetService, {
    computeDeployAddress: (_pk: PackedPublicKey) =>
      Effect.fail(new StarknetRpcError({ message: "connection refused", code: -1 })),
    getBalance: (_addr: string) =>
      Effect.fail(new StarknetRpcError({ message: "connection refused", code: -1 })),
    deployAccount: (_pk: PackedPublicKey, _sk: string, _salt: string) =>
      Effect.fail(new AccountDeployError({ message: "connection refused" })),
    waitForTx: (_tx: string) =>
      Effect.fail(new StarknetRpcError({ message: "timeout", code: -32003 })),

260→describe("StarknetService.computeDeployAddress — account-deploy step 3", () => {
   261→  it("success: returns a 0x-prefixed hex string (ContractAddress)", async () => {
   262→    const exit = await runWith(
   263→      StarknetService.computeDeployAddress(MOCK_PACKED_PK),
   264→      makeSuccessLayer(),
   265→    )
   266→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
   267→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
   268→      expect(typeof exit.value).toBe("string")
   269→      expect((exit.value as string).startsWith("0x")).toBe(true)
   270→    }
   271→  })
   272→
   273→  it("failure: StarknetRpcError carries code and message", async () => {
   274→    const exit = await runWith(
   275→      StarknetService.computeDeployAddress(MOCK_PACKED_PK),
   276→      makeRpcFailureLayer(),
   277→    )
   278→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   279→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
   280→      const failure = Cause.failureOption(exit.cause)
   281→      expect(failure._tag).toBe("Some")
   282→      // @ts-expect-error Option.Some
   283→      const err = failure.value
   284→      expect(err._tag).toBe("StarknetRpcError")
   285→      expect(err.code).toBe(-1)
   286→    }
   287→  })
   288→
   289→  it("real implementation (pure computation — no network): returns deterministic hex address", async () => {
   290→    const withTestConfig = ConfigProvider.fromMap(
   291→      new Map([["NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL", "http://localhost:9999"]]),
   292→    )
   293→    const exit = await Effect.runPromiseExit(
   294→      StarknetService.computeDeployAddress(MOCK_PACKED_PK).pipe(
   295→        Effect.provide(StarknetService.Default),
   296→        Effect.provide(Layer.setConfigProvider(withTestConfig)),
   297→      ),
   298→    )
   299→    // computeDeployAddress is pure (no RPC call), so must succeed
   300→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
   301→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
   302→      expect(typeof exit.value.address).toBe("string")
   303→      expect(exit.value.address.startsWith("0x")).toBe(true)
   304→      expect(typeof exit.value.salt).toBe("string")
   305→      expect(exit.value.salt.startsWith("0x")).toBe(true)
   306→    }
   307→  })
   308→})
   309→

364→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   365→
   366→describe("StarknetService.deployAccount — step 5", () => {
   367→  it("success: returns { txHash, address } with correct shapes", async () => {
   368→    const exit = await runWith(
   369→      StarknetService.deployAccount(MOCK_PACKED_PK, MOCK_PRIVATE_KEY),
   370→      makeSuccessLayer(),
   371→    )
   372→    expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
   373→    if (Exit.isSuccess(exit)) {
   374→      expect(typeof exit.value.txHash).toBe("string")
   375→      expect(typeof exit.value.address).toBe("string")
   376→      expect((exit.value.txHash as string).startsWith("0x")).toBe(true)
   377→      expect((exit.value.address as string).startsWith("0x")).toBe(true)
   378→    }
   379→  })
   380→
   381→  it("failure: AccountDeployError without txHash (pre-broadcast rejection)", async () => {
   382→    const exit = await runWith(
   383→      StarknetService.deployAccount(MOCK_PACKED_PK, MOCK_PRIVATE_KEY),
   384→      makeDeployFailureLayer(),
   385→    )
   386→    expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
   387→    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
   388→      const failure = Cause.failureOption(exit.cause)
   389→      // @ts-expect-error Option.Some
   390→      const err = failure.value
   391→      expect(err._tag).toBe("AccountDeployError")
   392→      expect(err.txHash).toBeUndefined()
   393→      expect(err.message).toBe("insufficient fee")
   394→    }
   395→  })
   396→

All tasks are complete. Here's the full summary:

---

## Sepolia Testnet Demo — Implementation Complete

**474 pass, 0 fail, typecheck clean, production build ✅**

### What was implemented

| Task | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| 1. Enable CASM compilation | ✅ | `casm = true`, CASM artifact built (1.1M) |
| 2. sncast.toml + declare script | ✅ | `sncast.toml` + `bin/declare-sepolia.sh` created |
| 3. Switch to Sepolia RPC | ✅ | `.env` + `.env.local` updated, StarknetService.ts comments updated |
| 4. Fix salt-reuse bug | ✅ | Salt threaded from `computeDeployAddress` → `deployAccount` |
| 5. Update UI copy + links | ✅ | mainnet→Sepolia, Starkscan→Voyager |
| 6. Faucet link + copy button + password input | ✅ | Fund step has copy-to-clipboard and faucet link |
| 7. Balance auto-polling | ✅ | 5s poll, shows balance + "Ready to deploy!" |
| 8. Wire packing into verification playground | ✅ | sign → pack → verify, 29 slots displayed |
| 9. Theme toggle | ✅ | Wired in layout |
| 10. Navigation header | ✅ | Sticky nav with Hero/Verify/Pipeline/Deploy anchors |
| 11. Tests + typecheck + build | ✅ | 474 pass, 0 fail, clean |
| 12. End-to-end manual test | **⚠️ Manual** | Requires funded Sepolia account (see below) |

---

### What you need to do manually

**To complete Task 2 (declare the contract) and Task 3 (fill in the class hash):**

```bash
# 1. Create/fund a Sepolia account if you don't have one
sncast account create --name sepolia-deployer --type oz
# Fund it at: https://starknet-faucet.vercel.app/
sncast account deploy --name sepolia-deployer --fee-token strk

# 2. Declare the contract
./bin/declare-sepolia.sh --account sepolia-deployer

# 3. Copy the class_hash output and update StarknetService.ts:
#    apps/demo/src/services/StarknetService.ts — FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH constant
```

Then the full deploy flow will be live on Sepolia.

# Brainstorming Ideas Into Designs

## Overview

Help turn ideas into fully formed designs and specs through natural collaborative dialogue.

Start by understanding the current project context, then ask questions one at a time to refine the idea. Once you understand what you're building, present the design and get user approval.

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code, scaffold any project, or take any implementation action until you have presented a design and the user has approved it. This applies to EVERY project regardless of perceived simplicity.
</HARD-GATE>

## Anti-Pattern: "This Is Too Simple To Need A Design"

Every project goes through this process. A todo list, a single-function utility, a config change — all of them. "Simple" projects are where unexamined assumptions cause the most wasted work. The design can be short (a few sentences for truly simple projects), but you MUST present it and get approval.

## Checklist

You MUST create a task for each of these items and complete them in order:

1. **Explore project context** — check files, docs, recent commits
2. **Ask clarifying questions** — one at a time, understand purpose/constraints/success criteria
3. **Propose 2-3 approaches** — with trade-offs and your recommendation
4. **Present design** — in sections scaled to their complexity, get user approval after each section
5. **Write design doc** — save to `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md` and commit
6. **Transition to implementation** — invoke writing-plans skill to create implementation plan

## Process Flow

```dot
digraph brainstorming {
    "Explore project context" [shape=box];
    "Ask clarifying questions" [shape=box];
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" [shape=box];
    "Present design sections" [shape=box];
    "User approves design?" [shape=diamond];
    "Write design doc" [shape=box];
    "Invoke writing-plans skill" [shape=doublecircle];

    "Explore project context" -> "Ask clarifying questions";
    "Ask clarifying questions" -> "Propose 2-3 approaches";
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" -> "Present design sections";
    "Present design sections" -> "User approves design?";
    "User approves design?" -> "Present design sections" [label="no, revise"];
    "User approves design?" -> "Write design doc" [label="yes"];
    "Write design doc" -> "Invoke writing-plans skill";
}
```

**The terminal state is invoking writing-plans.** Do NOT invoke frontend-design, mcp-builder, or any other implementation skill. The ONLY skill you invoke after brainstorming is writing-plans.

## The Process

**Understanding the idea:**
- Check out the current project state first (files, docs, recent commits)
- Ask questions one at a time to refine the idea
- Prefer multiple choice questions when possible, but open-ended is fine too
- Only one question per message - if a topic needs more exploration, break it into multiple questions
- Focus on understanding: purpose, constraints, success criteria

**Exploring approaches:**
- Propose 2-3 different approaches with trade-offs
- Present options conversationally with your recommendation and reasoning
- Lead with your recommended option and explain why

**Presenting the design:**
- Once you believe you understand what you're building, present the design
- Scale each section to its complexity: a few sentences if straightforward, up to 200-300 words if nuanced
- Ask after each section whether it looks right so far
- Cover: architecture, components, data flow, error handling, testing
- Be ready to go back and clarify if something doesn't make sense

## After the Design

**Documentation:**
- Write the validated design to `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md`
- Use elements-of-style:writing-clearly-and-concisely skill if available
- Commit the design document to git

**Implementation:**
- Invoke the writing-plans skill to create a detailed implementation plan
- Do NOT invoke any other skill. writing-plans is the next step.

## Key Principles

- **One question at a time** - Don't overwhelm with multiple questions
- **Multiple choice preferred** - Easier to answer than open-ended when possible
- **YAGNI ruthlessly** - Remove unnecessary features from all designs
- **Explore alternatives** - Always propose 2-3 approaches before settling
- **Incremental validation** - Present design, get approval before moving on
- **Be flexible** - Go back and clarify when something doesn't make sense

1→import { Config, Effect } from "effect"
     2→import { RpcProvider, Account, hash, CallData, stark } from "starknet"
     3→import {
     4→  StarknetRpcError,
     5→  AccountDeployError,
     6→} from "./errors"
     7→import { TxHash, ContractAddress } from "./types"
     8→import type { PackedPublicKey } from "./types"
     9→
    10→// FalconAccount class hash — declared on Starknet Sepolia
    11→// TODO: replace with actual class hash after running: ./bin/declare-sepolia.sh --account sepolia-deployer
    12→const FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH = "0x0"
    13→
    14→/** STRK token contract address (same on mainnet and Sepolia) */
    15→export const STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS =
    16→  "0x04718f5a0fc34cc1af16a1cdee98ffb20c31f5cd61d6ab07201858f4287c938d"
    17→
    18→export class StarknetService extends Effect.Service<StarknetService>()(
    19→  "StarknetService",
    20→  {
    21→    accessors: true,
    22→    effect: Effect.gen(function* () {
    23→      const rpcUrl = yield* Config.string("NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL")
    24→      const provider = new RpcProvider({ nodeUrl: rpcUrl })
    25→
    26→      const computeDeployAddress = Effect.fn("Starknet.computeDeployAddress")(
    27→        function* (packedPk: PackedPublicKey) {
    28→          const constructorCalldata = CallData.compile({
    29→            pk_packed: packedPk.slots,
    30→          })
    31→          const salt = stark.randomAddress()
    32→          const address = hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(
    33→            salt,
    34→            FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH,
    35→            constructorCalldata,
    36→            0,
    37→          )
    38→          return {
    39→            address: ContractAddress.make(address),
    40→            salt,
    41→          }
    42→        },
    43→      )
    44→
    45→      const getBalance = Effect.fn("Starknet.getBalance")(
    46→        function* (address: string) {
    47→          return yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    48→            try: async () => {
    49→              const result = await provider.callContract({
    50→                contractAddress: STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS,
    51→                entrypoint: "balanceOf",
    52→                calldata: [address],
    53→              })
    54→              return BigInt(result[0])
    55→            },
    56→            catch: (error) =>
    57→              new StarknetRpcError({ message: String(error), code: -1 }),
    58→          })
    59→        },
    60→      )
    61→
    62→      const deployAccount = Effect.fn("Starknet.deployAccount")(
    63→        function* (
    64→          packedPk: PackedPublicKey,
    65→          privateKey: string,
    66→          salt: string,
    67→        ) {
    68→          const constructorCalldata = CallData.compile({
    69→            pk_packed: packedPk.slots,
    70→          })
    71→          const address = hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(
    72→            salt,
    73→            FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH,
    74→            constructorCalldata,
    75→            0,
    76→          )
    77→
    78→          // starknet v9 Account constructor takes a single AccountOptions object
    79→          const account = new Account({ provider, address, signer: privateKey })
    80→
    81→          const result = yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    82→            try: () =>
    83→              account.deployAccount({
    84→                classHash: FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH,
    85→                constructorCalldata,
    86→                addressSalt: salt,
    87→                contractAddress: address,
    88→              }),
    89→            catch: (error) =>
    90→              new AccountDeployError({ message: String(error) }),
    91→          })
    92→
    93→          yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    94→            try: () => provider.waitForTransaction(result.transaction_hash),
    95→            catch: (error) =>
    96→              new AccountDeployError({
    97→                message: `Tx failed: ${error}`,
    98→                txHash: result.transaction_hash,
    99→              }),
   100→          })
   101→
   102→          return {
   103→            txHash: TxHash.make(result.transaction_hash),
   104→            address: ContractAddress.make(result.contract_address),
   105→          }
   106→        },
   107→      )
   108→
   109→      const waitForTx = Effect.fn("Starknet.waitForTx")(
   110→        function* (txHash: string) {
   111→          yield* Effect.tryPromise({
   112→            try: () => provider.waitForTransaction(txHash),
   113→            catch: (error) =>
   114→              new StarknetRpcError({ message: String(error), code: -1 }),
   115→          })
   116→        },
   117→      )
   118→
   119→      return {
   120→        computeDeployAddress,
   121→        getBalance,
   122→        deployAccount,
   123→        waitForTx,
   124→        provider,
   125→      }
   126→    }),
   127→  },
   128→) {}

Key observations:
1. `StarknetService` reads `NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL` from config at initialization time (when the Layer is built)
2. The `deployRuntime` is a module-level `ManagedRuntime` - it's built once when the module loads
3. The `FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH` is a module-level constant in `StarknetService.ts`
4. The faucet link is hardcoded in AccountDeployFlow.tsx


5. The explorer link is also hardcoded to `sepolia.voyager.online`

The real issue is that the service gets initialized once with the RPC URL from environment variables, so switching networks at runtime requires rethinking how the service is constructed. Let me check the page and layout files to see how everything ties together.

import { Footer } from "@/components/landing/Footer"
import { Hero } from "@/components/landing/Hero"
import { NavHeader } from "@/components/landing/NavHeader"
import { PerformanceStats } from "@/components/landing/PerformanceStats"
import { WhyPostQuantum } from "@/components/landing/WhyPostQuantum"
import { PlaygroundSection } from "@/components/interactive/PlaygroundSection"
import { PipelineSection } from "@/components/interactive/PipelineSection"
import { AccountDeploySection } from "@/components/interactive/AccountDeploySection"

export default function Home(): React.JSX.Element {
  return (
    <main className="min-h-screen">
      <NavHeader />
      <Hero />
      <WhyPostQuantum />
      <PerformanceStats />
      <PlaygroundSection />
      <PipelineSection />
      <AccountDeploySection />
      <Footer />
    </main>
  )
}

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture of the architecture. Let me think about the approaches for network switching.

**Key constraint:** `StarknetService` is an Effect service that reads `NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL` from Config at initialization time. The `ManagedRuntime` is built once at module load time in `AccountDeployFlow.tsx`. The `FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH` is a module-level constant.

To add network switching, I need to think about approaches.

**Approach 1: Environment Variable at Build Time (no runtime switching)**
- Add a build-time flag or separate build targets


- Not relevant here since the user wants seamless runtime switching

**Approach 2: Network Atom + Runtime Rebuild**
- Add a `networkAtom` that stores the current network (`"sepolia"` | `"mainnet"`)
- Store network config (RPC URL, class hash, explorer URL, isTestnet) per network as constants
- Pass the selected RPC URL as a prop or context to the StarknetService layer
- Rebuild the `ManagedRuntime` when network changes (or create two runtimes)
- The faucet link and explorer URL are computed from the network atom
- The challenge is that `StarknetService.Default` is initialized at module load time, so I'd need to either rebuild the runtime or inject the network config dynamically

**Approach 3: Config Override / Custom Layer**
- Define network configs as constants with Sepolia and Mainnet RPC URLs, class hashes, and explorer URLs
- Create a custom `StarknetService.Live` constructor that accepts the RPC URL and class hash as parameters
- Pass the selected network config to this layer and rebuild the ManagedRuntime when the network changes via a `useEffect` watching the atom
- The NavHeader displays a network toggle chip

Given the current architecture, the most practical approach would be to create a custom service layer that accepts network configuration at runtime rather than relying on static environment variables. content.text:
Good. Now I have enough context to propose approaches. Let me present them:

---

**The core challenge:** `StarknetService` reads the RPC URL from `Config.string("NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL")` at layer initialization time, and the `ManagedRuntime` is a module-level constant. Switching networks means rebuilding the runtime with new config. Also, `FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH` is a module-level constant — it needs to vary per network too (Sepolia vs Mainnet class hashes will differ once you declare on mainnet).

Here are 3 approaches:

**Option A — Network config atom + runtime rebuild (Recommended)**
- Add `networkAtom` storing `"sepolia" | "mainnet"` in `atoms/starknet.ts`
- Define both network configs as constants: `{ rpcUrl, classHash, explorerBaseUrl, isTestnet }`
- Change `StarknetService` to expose a `.make(rpcUrl, classHash)` layer factory instead of reading from `Config`
- `AccountDeployFlow` rebuilds its `ManagedRuntime` via `useEffect` when the network atom changes
- NavHeader reads the atom and renders a radio-style toggle — requires adding `"use client"` to it
- Faucet link hidden when `!networkConfig.isTestnet`
- **Pro:** Clean, explicit, proper network isolation. **Con:** Runtime rebuild complexity (but manageable — Effect's `ManagedRuntime` is lightweight for this use case)

**Option B — Keep env var config, page reload on switch**
- Network selection stored in `localStorage`
- On toggle, update `localStorage` and do `window.location.reload()`
- At load time, read from `localStorage` and `fetch` the right config via a server action
- **Pro:** Zero runtime complexity. **Con:** Page reload is jarring, loses in-progress deploy state

**Option C — Dual runtimes, always-alive**
- Create both `sepoliaRuntime` and `mainnetRuntime` at module level
- The atom just picks which one to use
- **Pro:** Instant switch, no rebuild. **Con:** Two live RPC connections, doubled cold-start cost — wasteful for a demo

**I recommend Option A** — it's the idiomatic Effect-TS approach, keeps the code clean, and the runtime rebuild is fast (no real resources to tear down, just an RPC provider object).

```typescript
export type NetworkId = "sepolia" | "mainnet"
export interface NetworkConfig {
  id: NetworkId
  name: string
  rpcUrl: string
  classHash: string           // 0x0 until declared per-network
  explorerBaseUrl: string     // sepolia.voyager.online vs voyager.online
  isTestnet: boolean
}
export const NETWORKS: Record<NetworkId, NetworkConfig> = {
  sepolia: {
    id: "sepolia", name: "Sepolia",
    rpcUrl: "https://api.zan.top/public/starknet-sepolia/rpc/v0_10",

  mainnet: {
    id: "mainnet", name: "Mainnet",
    rpcUrl: "https://api.zan.top/public/starknet/rpc/v0_10",
    classHash: "0x0",
    explorerBaseUrl: "https://voyager.online",
    isTestnet: false,
  },
}
```

content.text:
**Section 4: AccountDeployFlow changes**

These are the conditional behaviors driven by `networkConfig`:

| Element | Sepolia | Mainnet |
|---|---|---|
| Section description | "…Sepolia testnet…" | "…Mainnet…" |
| Faucet link in Fund step | ✅ Shown | ❌ Hidden |
| Balance display | ✅ Shown | ✅ Shown (you can still check balance on mainnet) |
| Explorer link | `sepolia.voyager.online/tx/…` | `voyager.online/tx/…` |
| Deploy step reset | On network switch | On network switch |

The RPC URL and class hash are passed to `StarknetService.make()` from the network config — so `computeDeployAddress` and `deployAccount` both use the correct values per network automatically.

That covers all 4 sections. Full design summary:
1. `src/config/networks.ts` — network config constants
2. `networkAtom` in `atoms/starknet.ts` — persisted to localStorage
3. `StarknetService.make(rpcUrl, classHash)` — custom layer factory, `Default` preserved
4. `NavHeader.tsx` — client component with pill toggle
5. `AccountDeployFlow.tsx` — runtime via ref, conditional faucet, dynamic explorer URL

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/felt/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/writing-plans

# Writing Plans

## Overview

Write comprehensive implementation plans assuming the engineer has zero context for our codebase and questionable taste. Document everything they need to know: which files to touch for each task, code, testing, docs they might need to check, how to test it. Give them the whole plan as bite-sized tasks. DRY. YAGNI. TDD. Frequent commits.

Assume they are a skilled developer, but know almost nothing about our toolset or problem domain. Assume they don't know good test design very well.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan."

**Context:** This should be run in a dedicated worktree (created by brainstorming skill).

**Save plans to:** `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md`

## Bite-Sized Task Granularity

**Each step is one action (2-5 minutes):**
- "Write the failing test" - step
- "Run it to make sure it fails" - step
- "Implement the minimal code to make the test pass" - step
- "Run the tests and make sure they pass" - step
- "Commit" - step

## Plan Document Header

**Every plan MUST start with this header:**

```markdown
# [Feature Name] Implementation Plan

> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** [One sentence describing what this builds]

**Architecture:** [2-3 sentences about approach]

**Tech Stack:** [Key technologies/libraries]

---
```

## Task Structure

````markdown
### Task N: [Component Name]

**Files:**
- Create: `exact/path/to/file.py`
- Modify: `exact/path/to/existing.py:123-145`
- Test: `tests/exact/path/to/test.py`
````

1→import { Config, Effect } from "effect"
     2→import { RpcProvider, Account, hash, CallData, stark } from "starknet"
     3→import {
     4→  StarknetRpcError,
     5→  AccountDeployError,
     6→} from "./errors"
     7→import { TxHash, ContractAddress } from "./types"
     8→import type { PackedPublicKey } from "./types"
     9→
    10→// FalconAccount class hash — declared on Starknet Sepolia
    11→// TODO: replace with actual class hash after running: ./bin/declare-sepolia.sh --account sepolia-deployer
    12→const FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH = "0x0"
    13→
    14→/** STRK token contract address (same on mainnet and Sepolia) */
    15→export const STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS =
    16→  "0x04718f5a0fc34cc1af16a1cdee98ffb20c31f5cd61d6ab07201858f4287c938d"
    17→
    18→export class StarknetService extends Effect.Service<StarknetService>()(
    19→  "StarknetService",
    20→  {
    21→    accessors: true,
    22→    effect: Effect.gen(function* () {
    23→      const rpcUrl = yield* Config.string("NEXT_PUBLIC_STARKNET_RPC_URL")
    24→      const provider = new RpcProvider({ nodeUrl: rpcUrl })
    25→
    26→      const computeDeployAddress = Effect.fn("Starknet.computeDeployAddress")(
    27→        function* (packedPk: PackedPublicKey) {
    28→          const constructorCalldata = CallData.compile({
    29→            pk_packed: packedPk.slots,
    30→          })
    31→          const salt = stark.randomAddress()
    32→          const address = hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(
    33→            salt,
    34→            FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH,
    35→            constructorCalldata,
    36→            0,
    37→          )
    38→          return {
    39→            address: ContractAddress.make(address),
    40→            salt,
    41→          }
    42→        },
    43→      )
    44→
    45→      const getBalance = Effect.fn("Starknet.getBalance")(
    46→        function* (address: string) {
    47→          return yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    48→            try: async () => {
    49→              const result = await provider.callContract({
    50→                contractAddress: STRK_TOKEN_ADDRESS,
    51→                entrypoint: "balanceOf",
    52→                calldata: [address],
    53→              })
    54→              return BigInt(result[0])
    55→            },
    56→            catch: (error) =>
    57→              new StarknetRpcError({ message: String(error), code: -1 }),
    58→          })
    59→        },
    60→      )
    61→
    62→      const deployAccount = Effect.fn("Starknet.deployAccount")(
    63→        function* (
    64→          packedPk: PackedPublicKey,
    65→          privateKey: string,
    66→          salt: string,
    67→        ) {
    68→          const constructorCalldata = CallData.compile({
    69→            pk_packed: packedPk.slots,
    70→          })
    71→          const address = hash.calculateContractAddressFromHash(
    72→            salt,
    73→            FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH,
    74→            constructorCalldata,
    75→            0,
    76→          )
    77→
    78→          // starknet v9 Account constructor takes a single AccountOptions object
    79→          const account = new Account({ provider, address, signer: privateKey })
    80→
    81→          const result = yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    82→            try: () =>
    83→              account.deployAccount({
    84→                classHash: FALCON_ACCOUNT_CLASS_HASH,
    85→                constructorCalldata,
    86→                addressSalt: salt,
    87→                contractAddress: address,
    88→              }),
    89→            catch: (error) =>
    90→              new AccountDeployError({ message: String(error) }),
    91→          })
    92→
    93→          yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    94→            try: () => provider.waitForTransaction(result.transaction_hash),
    95→            catch: (error) =>
    96→              new AccountDeployError({
    97→                message: `Tx failed: ${error}`,
    98→                txHash: result.transaction_hash,
    99→              }),
   100→          })
   101→
   102→          return {
   103→            txHash: TxHash.make(result.transaction_hash),
   104→            address: ContractAddress.make(result.contract_address),
   105→          }
   106→        },
   107→      )
   108→
   109→      const waitForTx = Effect.fn("Starknet.waitForTx")(
   110→        function* (txHash: string) {
   111→          yield* Effect.tryPromise({
   112→            try: () => provider.waitForTransaction(txHash),
   113→            catch: (error) =>
   114→              new StarknetRpcError({ message: String(error), code: -1 }),
   115→          })
   116→        },
   117→      )
   118→
   119→      return {
   120→        computeDeployAddress,
   121→        getBalance,
   122→        deployAccount,
   123→        waitForTx,
   124→        provider,
   125→      }
   126→    }),
   127→  },
   128→) {}
